Title: Account Activation Flow for PH and ID
Content:
KYC Flow: PH & ID Account Activation

Admin Dashboard Workflow

The Admin Dashboard is an interface used by MO Agents to perform the Know Your Customer (KYC) process for merchants onboarding with Xendit. KYC is an essential verification step that helps us confirm the identity, legitimacy, and risk profile of each merchant to meet regulatory requirements and protect our platform.

This section describes the complete Admin Dashboard workflow — from initial registration from the merchant side to how MO processes the merchant’s application.

The Admin Dashboard workflow includes the following steps:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

Merchant Account Activation

1

Merchant apply in Dashboard for account activation by following this steps: https://docs.xendit.co/docs/create-account

Merchant

MO Process Merchant’s Application

2

MO will process Account Activation request from Admin Dashboard > Onboarding Management > Select Country as either “Indonesia” or Philippines > Tab “Merchant Ops”

Merchant Ops

3

Firstly, agents will have to find merchant(s) with Risk Score: Very High Risk and proceed to decline this merchant without doing further KYC. Please refer to SOP: Merchant Risk Engine_v1

4

Click Merchant’s Profile to process the KYC based on the merchant’s request, then click Start KYC.

For Account Activation shift, please refer to: Regional Merchant Ops Execution Mapping

5

Please proceed with the KYC for merchants based on their entity type and submitted documents, and provide the corresponding KYC decision (LDD or BDD).

KYC Support & Escalation Actions

While performing KYC checks, MO Agents can use the available action buttons in the Admin Dashboard to escalate cases, request support, or flag merchants that require special attention. These actions help streamline cross-team collaboration and ensure that merchants are reviewed accurately and efficiently.

Below are the available actions and their respective purposes:

  1. Need MO prioritization: Agent on shift needs to prioritize this merchant
  2. Need Sales action: Agents can click the Flag Button to get more information or clarification from Merchant. Sales team will get notified on #onboarding-notif channel whenever this button is clicked.
  3. Need MR advice: Agents can click the Warning Sign Button to get advice from the Merchant-Risk Team, usually regarding the merchant's website. Mrisk team will get notified on #merchant-risk channel whenever this button is clicked.

Account Status and KYC Status

During merchant onboarding, the Admin Dashboard reflects two key indicators: Account Status and KYC Status. Together, these statuses provide visibility into the progress and outcome of the KYC process and guide MO Agents on how to proceed with each merchant case.

Use the table below as a reference to interpret each status combination and determine the appropriate next steps.

Account Status

KYC Status

Meaning

Next Step

AWAITING_DOCS

AWAITING_DATA

Merchant hasn’t completed all requirement for Account Activation

Merchant will need to complete required document/information

AWAITING_DOCS

INVALID

Agents have assessed merchant's application, but there’s document/info that is still missing/ required from merchant

Merchant will need to come back with the requested document/ information

PENDING_VERIFICATION

DATA_PROVIDED

Merchant already submitted the requirements, waiting to be KYC-ed

MO agents need to do KYC for this merchant

PENDING_VERIFICATION

PENDING

Merchant already submitted the requirements, but agents need more clarification from Xendit’s internal team (e.g: Sales/MRisk/ Compliance)

MO will need to follow up until case is solved and inform Sales/AM/Merchant for any additional requirements

DECLINED

FAILED

Merchant not pass KYC & can’t onboard with Xendit due to ineligibility in their application

LIVE

PASSED

Merchant passed KYC and has now onboarded with Xendit

Proceed to do channel activation (by request from merchant)

AWAITING_SIGN

PASSED

Merchant passed KYC but hasn’t signed Service Agreement

Info AM/Sales to notify merchant for signing Service Agreement in order to “Go-Live” (onboard with Xendit)

SUSPENDED

PASSED

Account already LIVE, but upon further investigation it is found that merchant is fraudulent, so their account is freeze / got suspended

Business Due Diligence

The Business Due Diligence (BDD) process guides MO Agents in verifying a merchant’s business information during onboarding. Agents review the merchant’s website, business description, and industry classification to ensure they align with Xendit’s policies and risk requirements.

Below are the general criteria a merchant's website must meet during the verification process:

  • The website or App must be ready, live, and accessible.
  • The website should match the nature of the business registered with Xendit
  • The website must reflect and match the contact information (such as business email, phone number, business address) registered with Xendit
  • We prefer websites and apps with catalogs or products with pricing details that are available to purchase via checkout flow.

Using the Admin Dashboard, agents determine whether the merchant meets the criteria to proceed, requires resubmission or clarification, or should be declined. The steps below outline the required actions and decision points for completing the BDD process consistently:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing Business Due Dilligince

1

Agents click on merchant’s websites and do KYC for their biz web. If the merchant's business description mismatch with the business website, please proceed to decline the merchant.

If otherwise, please continue to proceed with current SOPs as follows:

ttps://xendit.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/MR/pages/3477045652/Green+Flag+Check

For Entity Merchant with no website/online presence please follow No Website Alternative Initiative

MO

2

After doing assessment on merchant’s bizweb, if the website pass the criteria, please mark the website as good on Admin Dashboard.

If the website does not meet the paramater or invalid/inaccessible /incomplete/etc please mark the website as bad and request resubmission to the merchant. For reference, please refer to INVALID Websites SOP

3

Check for the merchant's Parent Industry and Child industry. If merchant chose the wrong Industry, please go to Edit (click pencil button on right-side) > Choose Parent Industry > Choose Child Industry > Save

Legal Due Diligence

The Legal Due Diligence (LDD) process enables MO Agents to verify the validity of legal documents submitted by merchants during onboarding. Agents review required documents in the Admin Dashboard and mark them as valid or request resubmission if invalid.

The steps below outline the standard workflow for completing LDD checks:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing Legal Due Dilligence

1

For LDD, what we need to make sure is whether documents that are submitted to us are legit. Agents can click Expand All and check if the required documents are legit or not (bank book scan is not needed to be checked anymore not unless needed e.g. High Risk Industry and if requested by Compliance).

2

After all the documents are legit, agents can mark documents as good. If otherwise, please mark the document as bad and proceed to request documents to the merchant.

3

Please do refer to Industry Specific Requirements (link) to check whether there’s additional documents required for certain business lines.

Or if in doubt, please make sure to the Compliance team by making a Slack post on #compliance-request and tag Ramie Ammar Bing Agor

Legal Documents Required for PH

The documents required to register with Xendit vary depending on your business type. To activate your account through the dashboard, you will need to upload the relevant legal documents for verification.

As a reference, the following are sample documents that merchants are required to submit during the onboarding process:

Document

Sample

Notes

Philsys/ePhilsys

  • Should show front and back page
  • Should show barcode
  • Should show photo of PhilSys / ePhilSys’s holder

Passport

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show passport holder’s specimen signature

Driver’s License

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show driver’s license holder’s specimen signature

SSS/GSIS

  • Should show photo of SSS / GSIS’s holder
  • Should show SSS / GSIS’s specimen signature

PRC ID

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show photo of PRC ID’s holder

Postal ID

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show photo of postal ID’s holder
  • Should show postal ID holder’s specimen signature

Voter’s ID

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show passport holder’s specimen signature

UMID

  • Should show photo of UMID’s holder
  • Should show UMID holder’s specimen signature

ACR/Immigration COR

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show passport holder’s specimen signature

Selfie with ID

  • Should show full face
  • Should show full ID (if selfie with ID)
  • Should put ID below or side-by-side your face (if selfie with ID)
  • Selfie should match with photo of ID

BIR 2303

Scan from hard-copy:

  • BIR2303 must indicate/align with the merchant's legal name in AoI/SEC/DTI
  • Must be submitted in complete pages (see the page number in the footer)

e-BIR:

  • E-BIR2303 must indicate/align with the merchant's legal name in AoI/SEC/DTI
  • Must be submitted in complete pages (see the page number in the footer)
  • Make sure it has QR code and MO will have to scan it as it will redirect across the database, showing merchant’s detail

DTI Certificate of Registration

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should not be expired
  • Should show full page of DTI

You may verify merchant’s certificate of registration from DTI BNRS

  • Guide on How to verify DTI Certificate

SEC Certificate of Registration

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should not be expired
  • Should show full page of SEC

You may verify the validity of a document in SEC Database to check for license status. If result is:

  • Registered - can continue with BAU process (BAU KYC)
  • Revoked - Decline the application / raise to Compliance if merchant looks legit
  • Suspended - Decline the application / raise to Compliance if merchant looks legit
  • Delinquent - proceed with EDD

Article of Incorporation

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should show full pages of AoI
  • Should be signed and notarized

Article of Partnership

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should show full pages of AoP
  • Should be signed and notarized

General Information Sheet (GIS)

  • should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • should be the most recent one
  • should show full pages of GIS for at least 9 pages
  • should be signed and notarized

PH Notarized Secretary’s or Partner’s Certificate

Secretary's Certificate [Template]

Partnership Secretary's Certificate [template]

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name and address stated in the application
  • Should authorize the person to apply and secure the services of Xendit Philippines, Inc or in general
  • Should be signed and notarized

For more information, please refer to this SOP: PH Legal Due Diligence Requirements [Restricted]

Legal Documents Required for ID

The documents required to register with Xendit vary depending on your business type. To activate your account through the dashboard, you will need to upload the relevant legal documents for verification.

As a reference, the following are sample documents that merchants are required to submit during the onboarding process:

Document

Sample

Notes

Authorized Person's KTP

  • KTP must belong to one of the directors stated in Akta Pendirian
  • Photo and details must be visible and readable
  • KTP must be valid

Corporate NPWP (Tax ID)

  • KTP must belong to one of the directors stated in your Akta Pendirian
  • Photo and details must be visible and readable
  • KTP must be valid

Authorized Person's KITAS

  • KITAS must belong to one of the directors stated in Akta Pendirian
  • Photo and details must be visible and readable
  • KITAS should be still active

NIB

NIB

NIB Guidelines:

  • The status of your NIB must be 'Terbit'
  • The first page must include all business details (i.e. business name, business address, contact details, etc.)
  • The next page(s) must contain the KBLI list (if applicable)
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Deed of Latest Change (Akta pengangkatan Direktur Terakhir)

Akta

  • Name in document must match the director's name
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Decree with the Latest Board of Director (SK Menkeh Akta Pengangkatan Direktur Terakhir)

SK Menkeh

  • Business name must match the one stated in your Akta Pendirian
  • Document must contain director details
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Surat Pernyataan Pendirian Perseroan Perorangan

  • Legal name written on the document should match the data registered in Xendit
  • Director name should match the data registered in Xendit

Sertifikat Pendaftaran Pendirian Perseroan Perorangan

  • Legal name written on the document should match the data registered in Xendit

Other business-specific license

  • For specific industry types, there will be additional license needed for activation
  • Kindly refer to documentation here

Proof of Business

List of accepted Proof of Business:

  • Website
  • Social Media
  • Photo of Offline Store
  • Poster / Brochure / Pamphlet
  • Invoice / Purchase Order (PO)
  • Bank Transfer Proof

PEP (Politically Exposed Person)

PEP (Politically Exposed Person) screening is a critical component of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) regulation. It involves verifying a merchant’s identity to determine whether they, or their related parties, are classified as Politically Exposed Persons.

For more information related to PEP flow, kindly refer to documentation here: PEP Flow

Onboarding Decision

After completing the BDD and LDD reviews, an onboarding decision is made based on the merchant’s eligibility and verification results. This decision determines the next action required to move the merchant forward in the onboarding process.

There are three possible onboarding outcomes, each with its own workflow and follow-up steps:

Requesting Resubmission

The resubmission process is used when a merchant’s application or documents do not fully meet the required criteria but can be corrected. Agents request the merchant to update or re-upload specific information or documents through the dashboard.

This flow outlines when to request resubmission and the steps agents should follow to ensure the required updates are completed:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

1

MO mark KYC requirements as bad or good

MO

2

MO put notes on the reason why document mark as bad in the Note. Please pick on MO agent name, fill in the notes, and also check every note history when doing KYC. And then proceed to click Save.

3

Proceed to request to merchant by clicking Action and Request Resubmission

4

Please select the Request Reason. Documents marked as “Bad” will automatically appear in the list of documents for resubmission.

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant pass KYC, mark all documents & website parameters as good - as seen on image below

And then click SAVE

2

Click PASS button

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS
  • AML
  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD

Steps to Reject Merchant Application:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

After doing KYC

1

MO need to put notes on why this merchant can’t pass KYC

And then click Save

MO

2

Choose “FAIL” as KYC Decision

And then, choose Rejection Reason

Click SAVE

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

MO mark KYC requirements as bad or good

MO

2

MO put notes on the reason why document mark as bad in the Note. Please pick on MO agent name, fill in the notes, and also check every note history when doing KYC. And then proceed to click Save.

3

Proceed to request to merchant by clicking Action and Request Resubmission

4

Please select the Request Reason. Documents marked as “Bad” will automatically appear in the list of documents for resubmission.

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant pass KYC, mark all documents & website parameters as good - as seen on image below

And then click SAVE

2

Click PASS button

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS
  • AML
  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD

Steps to Reject Merchant Application:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

After doing KYC

1

MO need to put notes on why this merchant can’t pass KYC

And then click Save

MO

2

Choose “FAIL” as KYC Decision

And then, choose Rejection Reason

Click SAVE
SeoTitle: Account Activation Flow for PH and ID

Title: Account Activation Flow for PH and ID
Content:
KYC Flow: PH & ID Account Activation

Admin Dashboard Workflow

The Admin Dashboard is an interface used by MO Agents to perform the Know Your Customer (KYC) process for merchants onboarding with Xendit. KYC is an essential verification step that helps us confirm the identity, legitimacy, and risk profile of each merchant to meet regulatory requirements and protect our platform.

This section describes the complete Admin Dashboard workflow — from initial registration from the merchant side to how MO processes the merchant’s application.

The Admin Dashboard workflow includes the following steps:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

Merchant Account Activation

1

Merchant apply in Dashboard for account activation by following this steps: https://docs.xendit.co/docs/create-account

Merchant

MO Process Merchant’s Application

2

MO will process Account Activation request from Admin Dashboard > Onboarding Management > Select Country as either “Indonesia” or Philippines > Tab “Merchant Ops”

Merchant Ops

3

Firstly, agents will have to find merchant(s) with Risk Score: Very High Risk and proceed to decline this merchant without doing further KYC. Please refer to SOP: Merchant Risk Engine_v1

4

Click Merchant’s Profile to process the KYC based on the merchant’s request, then click Start KYC.

For Account Activation shift, please refer to: Regional Merchant Ops Execution Mapping

5

Please proceed with the KYC for merchants based on their entity type and submitted documents, and provide the corresponding KYC decision (LDD or BDD).

KYC Support & Escalation Actions

While performing KYC checks, MO Agents can use the available action buttons in the Admin Dashboard to escalate cases, request support, or flag merchants that require special attention. These actions help streamline cross-team collaboration and ensure that merchants are reviewed accurately and efficiently.

Below are the available actions and their respective purposes:

  1. Need MO prioritization: Agent on shift needs to prioritize this merchant
  2. Need Sales action: Agents can click the Flag Button to get more information or clarification from Merchant. Sales team will get notified on #onboarding-notif channel whenever this button is clicked.
  3. Need MR advice: Agents can click the Warning Sign Button to get advice from the Merchant-Risk Team, usually regarding the merchant's website. Mrisk team will get notified on #merchant-risk channel whenever this button is clicked.

Account Status and KYC Status

During merchant onboarding, the Admin Dashboard reflects two key indicators: Account Status and KYC Status. Together, these statuses provide visibility into the progress and outcome of the KYC process and guide MO Agents on how to proceed with each merchant case.

Use the table below as a reference to interpret each status combination and determine the appropriate next steps.

Account Status

KYC Status

Meaning

Next Step

AWAITING_DOCS

AWAITING_DATA

Merchant hasn’t completed all requirement for Account Activation

Merchant will need to complete required document/information

AWAITING_DOCS

INVALID

Agents have assessed merchant's application, but there’s document/info that is still missing/ required from merchant

Merchant will need to come back with the requested document/ information

PENDING_VERIFICATION

DATA_PROVIDED

Merchant already submitted the requirements, waiting to be KYC-ed

MO agents need to do KYC for this merchant

PENDING_VERIFICATION

PENDING

Merchant already submitted the requirements, but agents need more clarification from Xendit’s internal team (e.g: Sales/MRisk/ Compliance)

MO will need to follow up until case is solved and inform Sales/AM/Merchant for any additional requirements

DECLINED

FAILED

Merchant not pass KYC & can’t onboard with Xendit due to ineligibility in their application

LIVE

PASSED

Merchant passed KYC and has now onboarded with Xendit

Proceed to do channel activation (by request from merchant)

AWAITING_SIGN

PASSED

Merchant passed KYC but hasn’t signed Service Agreement

Info AM/Sales to notify merchant for signing Service Agreement in order to “Go-Live” (onboard with Xendit)

SUSPENDED

PASSED

Account already LIVE, but upon further investigation it is found that merchant is fraudulent, so their account is freeze / got suspended

Business Due Diligence

The Business Due Diligence (BDD) process guides MO Agents in verifying a merchant’s business information during onboarding. Agents review the merchant’s website, business description, and industry classification to ensure they align with Xendit’s policies and risk requirements.

Below are the general criteria a merchant's website must meet during the verification process:

  • The website or App must be ready, live, and accessible.
  • The website should match the nature of the business registered with Xendit
  • The website must reflect and match the contact information (such as business email, phone number, business address) registered with Xendit
  • We prefer websites and apps with catalogs or products with pricing details that are available to purchase via checkout flow.

Using the Admin Dashboard, agents determine whether the merchant meets the criteria to proceed, requires resubmission or clarification, or should be declined. The steps below outline the required actions and decision points for completing the BDD process consistently:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing Business Due Dilligince

1

Agents click on merchant’s websites and do KYC for their biz web. If the merchant's business description mismatch with the business website, please proceed to decline the merchant.

If otherwise, please continue to proceed with current SOPs as follows:

ttps://xendit.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/MR/pages/3477045652/Green+Flag+Check

For Entity Merchant with no website/online presence please follow No Website Alternative Initiative

MO

2

After doing assessment on merchant’s bizweb, if the website pass the criteria, please mark the website as good on Admin Dashboard.

If the website does not meet the paramater or invalid/inaccessible /incomplete/etc please mark the website as bad and request resubmission to the merchant. For reference, please refer to INVALID Websites SOP

3

Check for the merchant's Parent Industry and Child industry. If merchant chose the wrong Industry, please go to Edit (click pencil button on right-side) > Choose Parent Industry > Choose Child Industry > Save

Legal Due Diligence

The Legal Due Diligence (LDD) process enables MO Agents to verify the validity of legal documents submitted by merchants during onboarding. Agents review required documents in the Admin Dashboard and mark them as valid or request resubmission if invalid.

The steps below outline the standard workflow for completing LDD checks:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing Legal Due Dilligence

1

For LDD, what we need to make sure is whether documents that are submitted to us are legit. Agents can click Expand All and check if the required documents are legit or not (bank book scan is not needed to be checked anymore not unless needed e.g. High Risk Industry and if requested by Compliance).

2

After all the documents are legit, agents can mark documents as good. If otherwise, please mark the document as bad and proceed to request documents to the merchant.

3

Please do refer to Industry Specific Requirements (link) to check whether there’s additional documents required for certain business lines.

Or if in doubt, please make sure to the Compliance team by making a Slack post on #compliance-request and tag Ramie Ammar Bing Agor

Legal Documents Required for PH

The documents required to register with Xendit vary depending on your business type. To activate your account through the dashboard, you will need to upload the relevant legal documents for verification.

As a reference, the following are sample documents that merchants are required to submit during the onboarding process:

Document

Sample

Notes

Philsys/ePhilsys

  • Should show front and back page
  • Should show barcode
  • Should show photo of PhilSys / ePhilSys’s holder

Passport

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show passport holder’s specimen signature

Driver’s License

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show driver’s license holder’s specimen signature

SSS/GSIS

  • Should show photo of SSS / GSIS’s holder
  • Should show SSS / GSIS’s specimen signature

PRC ID

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show photo of PRC ID’s holder

Postal ID

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show photo of postal ID’s holder
  • Should show postal ID holder’s specimen signature

Voter’s ID

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show passport holder’s specimen signature

UMID

  • Should show photo of UMID’s holder
  • Should show UMID holder’s specimen signature

ACR/Immigration COR

  • Should not be expired
  • Should show passport holder’s specimen signature

Selfie with ID

  • Should show full face
  • Should show full ID (if selfie with ID)
  • Should put ID below or side-by-side your face (if selfie with ID)
  • Selfie should match with photo of ID

BIR 2303

Scan from hard-copy:

  • BIR2303 must indicate/align with the merchant's legal name in AoI/SEC/DTI
  • Must be submitted in complete pages (see the page number in the footer)

e-BIR:

  • E-BIR2303 must indicate/align with the merchant's legal name in AoI/SEC/DTI
  • Must be submitted in complete pages (see the page number in the footer)
  • Make sure it has QR code and MO will have to scan it as it will redirect across the database, showing merchant’s detail

DTI Certificate of Registration

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should not be expired
  • Should show full page of DTI

You may verify merchant’s certificate of registration from DTI BNRS

  • Guide on How to verify DTI Certificate

SEC Certificate of Registration

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should not be expired
  • Should show full page of SEC

You may verify the validity of a document in SEC Database to check for license status. If result is:

  • Registered - can continue with BAU process (BAU KYC)
  • Revoked - Decline the application / raise to Compliance if merchant looks legit
  • Suspended - Decline the application / raise to Compliance if merchant looks legit
  • Delinquent - proceed with EDD

Article of Incorporation

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should show full pages of AoI
  • Should be signed and notarized

Article of Partnership

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • Should show full pages of AoP
  • Should be signed and notarized

General Information Sheet (GIS)

  • should match merchant’s legal business name stated in the application
  • should be the most recent one
  • should show full pages of GIS for at least 9 pages
  • should be signed and notarized

PH Notarized Secretary’s or Partner’s Certificate

Secretary's Certificate [Template]

Partnership Secretary's Certificate [template]

  • Should match merchant’s legal business name and address stated in the application
  • Should authorize the person to apply and secure the services of Xendit Philippines, Inc or in general
  • Should be signed and notarized

For more information, please refer to this SOP: PH Legal Due Diligence Requirements [Restricted]

Legal Documents Required for ID

The documents required to register with Xendit vary depending on your business type. To activate your account through the dashboard, you will need to upload the relevant legal documents for verification.

As a reference, the following are sample documents that merchants are required to submit during the onboarding process:

Document

Sample

Notes

Authorized Person's KTP

  • KTP must belong to one of the directors stated in Akta Pendirian
  • Photo and details must be visible and readable
  • KTP must be valid

Corporate NPWP (Tax ID)

  • KTP must belong to one of the directors stated in your Akta Pendirian
  • Photo and details must be visible and readable
  • KTP must be valid

Authorized Person's KITAS

  • KITAS must belong to one of the directors stated in Akta Pendirian
  • Photo and details must be visible and readable
  • KITAS should be still active

NIB

NIB

NIB Guidelines:

  • The status of your NIB must be 'Terbit'
  • The first page must include all business details (i.e. business name, business address, contact details, etc.)
  • The next page(s) must contain the KBLI list (if applicable)
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Deed of Latest Change (Akta pengangkatan Direktur Terakhir)

Akta

  • Name in document must match the director's name
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Decree with the Latest Board of Director (SK Menkeh Akta Pengangkatan Direktur Terakhir)

SK Menkeh

  • Business name must match the one stated in your Akta Pendirian
  • Document must contain director details
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Surat Pernyataan Pendirian Perseroan Perorangan

  • Legal name written on the document should match the data registered in Xendit
  • Director name should match the data registered in Xendit

Sertifikat Pendaftaran Pendirian Perseroan Perorangan

  • Legal name written on the document should match the data registered in Xendit

Other business-specific license

  • For specific industry types, there will be additional license needed for activation
  • Kindly refer to documentation here

Proof of Business

List of accepted Proof of Business:

  • Website
  • Social Media
  • Photo of Offline Store
  • Poster / Brochure / Pamphlet
  • Invoice / Purchase Order (PO)
  • Bank Transfer Proof

PEP (Politically Exposed Person)

PEP (Politically Exposed Person) screening is a critical component of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) regulation. It involves verifying a merchant’s identity to determine whether they, or their related parties, are classified as Politically Exposed Persons.

For more information related to PEP flow, kindly refer to documentation here: PEP Flow

Onboarding Decision

After completing the BDD and LDD reviews, an onboarding decision is made based on the merchant’s eligibility and verification results. This decision determines the next action required to move the merchant forward in the onboarding process.

There are three possible onboarding outcomes, each with its own workflow and follow-up steps:

Requesting Resubmission

The resubmission process is used when a merchant’s application or documents do not fully meet the required criteria but can be corrected. Agents request the merchant to update or re-upload specific information or documents through the dashboard.

This flow outlines when to request resubmission and the steps agents should follow to ensure the required updates are completed:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

1

MO mark KYC requirements as bad or good

MO

2

MO put notes on the reason why document mark as bad in the Note. Please pick on MO agent name, fill in the notes, and also check every note history when doing KYC. And then proceed to click Save.

3

Proceed to request to merchant by clicking Action and Request Resubmission

4

Please select the Request Reason. Documents marked as “Bad” will automatically appear in the list of documents for resubmission.

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant pass KYC, mark all documents & website parameters as good - as seen on image below

And then click SAVE

2

Click PASS button

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS
  • AML
  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD

Steps to Reject Merchant Application:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

After doing KYC

1

MO need to put notes on why this merchant can’t pass KYC

And then click Save

MO

2

Choose “FAIL” as KYC Decision

And then, choose Rejection Reason

Click SAVE

Deed of Latest Change (Akta pengangkatan Direktur Terakhir)

Akta

  • Name in document must match the director's name
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Decree with the Latest Board of Director (SK Menkeh Akta Pengangkatan Direktur Terakhir)

SK Menkeh

  • Business name must match the one stated in your Akta Pendirian
  • Document must contain director details
  • Make sure you upload all pages of the document

Surat Pernyataan Pendirian Perseroan Perorangan

  • Legal name written on the document should match the data registered in Xendit
  • Director name should match the data registered in Xendit

Sertifikat Pendaftaran Pendirian Perseroan Perorangan

  • Legal name written on the document should match the data registered in Xendit

Other business-specific license

  • For specific industry types, there will be additional license needed for activation
  • Kindly refer to documentation here

Proof of Business

List of accepted Proof of Business:

  • Website
  • Social Media
  • Photo of Offline Store
  • Poster / Brochure / Pamphlet
  • Invoice / Purchase Order (PO)
  • Bank Transfer Proof

PEP (Politically Exposed Person)

PEP (Politically Exposed Person) screening is a critical component of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) regulation. It involves verifying a merchant’s identity to determine whether they, or their related parties, are classified as Politically Exposed Persons.

For more information related to PEP flow, kindly refer to documentation here: PEP Flow

Onboarding Decision

After completing the BDD and LDD reviews, an onboarding decision is made based on the merchant’s eligibility and verification results. This decision determines the next action required to move the merchant forward in the onboarding process.

There are three possible onboarding outcomes, each with its own workflow and follow-up steps:

Requesting Resubmission

The resubmission process is used when a merchant’s application or documents do not fully meet the required criteria but can be corrected. Agents request the merchant to update or re-upload specific information or documents through the dashboard.

This flow outlines when to request resubmission and the steps agents should follow to ensure the required updates are completed:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

MO Doing KYC

1

MO mark KYC requirements as bad or good

MO

2

MO put notes on the reason why document mark as bad in the Note. Please pick on MO agent name, fill in the notes, and also check every note history when doing KYC. And then proceed to click Save.

3

Proceed to request to merchant by clicking Action and Request Resubmission

4

Please select the Request Reason. Documents marked as “Bad” will automatically appear in the list of documents for resubmission.

Activation Process

The activation process applies to merchants who have successfully passed all verification checks. Once approved, agents proceed with activating the merchant’s account so they can start using Xendit services.

This section explains the steps required to activate an account and any follow-up actions needed before the merchant can go live:

Category

No

Steps

MO Doing KYC

1

After doing KYC consist of BDD & LDD, and merchant pass KYC, mark all documents & website parameters as good - as seen on image below

And then click SAVE

2

Click PASS button

Rejection Flow

The rejection flow is used when a merchant does not meet Xendit’s eligibility, compliance, or risk requirements and cannot proceed with onboarding. This decision is final and prevents the account from being activated.

This section describes the criteria for rejection and the steps agents should take to reject the request:

Rejection Scenarios:

Category

Scenario

Next Step

Risk Engine

Merchant categorized as Very High Risk Merchant

Decline with reason SUSPECTED_FRAUD

LDD

Merchant submits all bad or random docs

Decline with reason FAILED_KYC

Business is in a prohibited/restricted category.

Decline with reason INELIGIBLE

BDD

Business in the blacklist / sanctions / anti money laundering (AML) category

Decline with reason

  • SANCTIONS
  • AML
  • BLACKLIST

Detected suspicious activity in your account

Decline with reason FRAUD

Some details in business proof (website/apps) are invalid or do not meet the requirements

Decline with reason FAILED_BDD

Steps to Reject Merchant Application:

Category

No

Steps

Owner

After doing KYC

1

MO need to put notes on why this merchant can’t pass KYC

And then click Save

MO

2

Choose “FAIL” as KYC Decision

And then, choose Rejection Reason

Click SAVE
